summary: defi-threat-scanner exfiltrates host data and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package's lifecycle script (referenced from package.json line 32) and scanner.js together form a system-information exfiltration payload. scanner.js requires os, https, fs, and child_process, then collects host identifiers via os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), process.cwd(), and filesystem existence checks against local paths, and POSTs the resulting data to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced from scanner.js lines 143/161/323 and from package.json line 32). The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site unrelated to any plausible publisher of a 'defi threat scanner' tool, with no version pinning, no integrity check, and additional curl invocations (lines 298/323/408) that further beacon to the same host. The combination of hardcoded C2, host/identity collection (hostname, whoami, cwd, env), and network egress at install/require time matches the active-attack credential-and-host-recon fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
